TITLE: Budgeting Basics

CATEGORY: Budgeting

SUMMARY: Budgeting is planning income and expenses to achieve financial goals. It provides control over spending and enables systematic wealth building.

KEY POINTS:
- Track income and expenses
- Allocate funds to categories
- Control discretionary spending
- Achieve financial goals
- Identify spending patterns
- Build financial discipline

BUDGET CATEGORIES:
- Fixed expenses: Rent, insurance, EMI
- Variable expenses: Food, utilities, transport
- Discretionary: Entertainment, dining, shopping
- Savings: Emergency fund, investments
- Debt repayment: Loan EMI, credit card

50-30-20 RULE:
- 50% for needs (rent, food, utilities)
- 30% for wants (entertainment, dining)
- 20% for savings and debt repayment

TITLE: Credit Card Debt Management

CATEGORY: Debt Management

SUMMARY: Credit card debt carries high interest rates (18-45% annually). Effective management requires understanding charges, paying on time, and strategic repayment to avoid debt spiral.

gement requires understanding charges, paying on time, and strategic repayment to avoid debt spiral.

KEY POINTS:
- Interest rates: 18-45% per annum
- Minimum payment: 5% of outstanding balance
- Grace period: 20-50 days (if full payment made)
- Late payment charges: Rs. 100-500
- Annual fees: Rs. 500-5,000
- Credit limit: Based on income and credit score

TITLE: Debt Repayment Methods

CATEGORY: Debt Management

SUMMARY: Different debt repayment strategies suit different situations. Choosing the right method accelerates debt freedom and minimizes total interest paid.

KEY POINTS:
- Debt snowball: Pay smallest debt first
- Debt avalanche: Pay highest interest first
- Balanced approach: Mix of both methods
- Prepayment reduces total interest
- Refinancing lowers interest rates

TITLE: EMI Management and Planning

CATEGORY: Debt Management

SUMMARY: EMI (Equated Monthly Installment) should not exceed 40-50% of monthly income. Proper planning ensures financial stability while managing loan obligations.

KEY POINTS:
- EMI formula: [P × R × (1+R)^N] / [(1+R)^N - 1]
- Safe limit: 40-50% of monthly income
- Includes all loan EMIs combined
- Fixed or floating interest rates
- Prepayment reduces total interest
- Affects credit score if missed

EQUATED MONTHLY INSTALLMENT (EMI) GUIDELINES

What is EMI?
An Equated Monthly Installment (EMI) is a fixed payment amount made by a borrower to a lender at a specified date each calendar month. EMIs are used to pay off both loans and mortgages over a set period.

ified date each calendar month. EMIs are used to pay off both loans and mortgages over a set period.

EMI Formula:
EMI = [P x R x (1+R)^N]/[(1+R)^N-1]
Where:
P = Principal loan amount
R = Monthly interest rate (annual rate/12)
N = Number of monthly installments
